verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचइति ते वर्णित: क्षत्त: कालाख्य: परमात्मन: ।महिमा वेदगर्भोऽथ यथास्राक्षीन्निबोध मे ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaiti te varṇitaḥ kṣattaḥkālākhyaḥ paramātmanaḥmahimā veda-garbho ’thayathāsrākṣīn nibodha me
verses.text: Śrī Maitreya said: O learned Vidura, so far I have explained to you the glories of the form of the Supreme Personality of Godhead in His feature of kāla. Now you can hear from me about the creation of Brahmā, the reservoir of all Vedic knowledge.

verses.sanskrit: ससर्जाग्रेऽन्धतामिस्रमथ तामिस्रमादिकृत् ।महामोहं च मोहं च तमश्चाज्ञानवृत्तय: ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: sasarjāgre ’ndha-tāmisramatha tāmisram ādi-kṛtmahāmohaṁ ca mohaṁ catamaś cājñāna-vṛttayaḥ
verses.text: Brahmā first created the nescient engagements like self-deception, the sense of death, anger after frustration, the sense of false ownership, and the illusory bodily conception, or forgetfulness of one’s real identity.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा पापीयसीं सृष्टिं नात्मानं बह्वमन्यत ।भगवद्ध्यानपूतेन मनसान्यां ततोऽसृजत् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā pāpīyasīṁ sṛṣṭiṁnātmānaṁ bahv amanyatabhagavad-dhyāna-pūtenamanasānyāṁ tato ’sṛjat
verses.text: Seeing such a misleading creation as a sinful task, Brahmā did not feel much pleasure in his activity, and therefore he purified himself by meditation on the Personality of Godhead. Then he began another term of creation.

verses.sanskrit: सनकं च सनन्दं च सनातनमथात्मभू: ।सनत्कुमारं च मुनीन्निष्क्रियानूर्ध्वरेतस: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sanakaṁ ca sanandaṁ casanātanam athātmabhūḥsanat-kumāraṁ ca munīnniṣkriyān ūrdhva-retasaḥ
verses.text: In the beginning, Brahmā created four great sages named Sanaka, Sananda, Sanātana and Sanat-kumāra. All of them were unwilling to adopt materialistic activities because they were highly elevated due to their semen’s flowing upwards.

verses.sanskrit: तान् बभाषे स्वभू: पुत्रान् प्रजा: सृजत पुत्रका: ।तन्नैच्छन्मोक्षधर्माणो वासुदेवपरायणा: ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tān babhāṣe svabhūḥ putrānprajāḥ sṛjata putrakāḥtan naicchan mokṣa-dharmāṇovāsudeva-parāyaṇāḥ
verses.text: Brahmā spoke to his sons after generating them. “My dear sons,” he said, “now generate progeny.” But due to their being attached to Vāsudeva, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, they aimed at liberation, and therefore they expressed their unwillingness.

verses.sanskrit: सोऽवध्यात: सुतैरेवं प्रत्याख्यातानुशासनै: ।क्रोधं दुर्विषहं जातं नियन्तुमुपचक्रमे ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: so ’vadhyātaḥ sutair evaṁpratyākhyātānuśāsanaiḥkrodhaṁ durviṣahaṁ jātaṁniyantum upacakrame
verses.text: On the refusal of the sons to obey the order of their father, there was much anger generated in the mind of Brahmā, which he tried to control and not express.

verses.sanskrit: धिया निगृह्यमाणोऽपि भ्रुवोर्मध्यात्प्रजापते: ।सद्योऽजायत तन्मन्यु: कुमारो नीललोहित: ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhiyā nigṛhyamāṇo ’pibhruvor madhyāt prajāpateḥsadyo ’jāyata tan-manyuḥkumāro nīla-lohitaḥ
verses.text: Although he tried to curb his anger, it came out from between his eyebrows, and a child mixed blue and red was immediately generated.

verses.sanskrit: स वै रुरोद देवानां पूर्वजो भगवान् भव: ।नामानि कुरु मे धात: स्थानानि च जगद्गुरो ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vai ruroda devānāṁpūrvajo bhagavān bhavaḥnāmāni kuru me dhātaḥsthānāni ca jagad-guro
verses.text: After his birth he began to cry: O destiny maker, teacher of the universe, kindly designate my name and place.

verses.sanskrit: इति तस्य वच: पाद्मो भगवान् परिपालयन् ।अभ्यधाद्भद्रया वाचा मा रोदीस्तत्करोमि ते ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti tasya vacaḥ pādmobhagavān paripālayanabhyadhād bhadrayā vācāmā rodīs tat karomi te
verses.text: The all-powerful Brahmā, who was born from the lotus flower, pacified the boy with gentle words, accepting his request, and said: Do not cry. I shall certainly do as you desire.

verses.sanskrit: यदरोदी: सुरश्रेष्ठ सोद्वेग इव बालक: ।ततस्त्वामभिधास्यन्ति नाम्ना रुद्र इति प्रजा: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: yad arodīḥ sura-śreṣṭhasodvega iva bālakaḥtatas tvām abhidhāsyantināmnā rudra iti prajāḥ
verses.text: Thereafter Brahmā said: O chief of the demigods, you shall be called by the name Rudra by all people because you have so anxiously cried.

verses.sanskrit: हृदिन्द्रियाण्यसुर्व्योम वायुरग्निर्जलं मही ।सूर्यश्चन्द्रस्तपश्चैव स्थानान्यग्रे कृतानि ते ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: hṛd indriyāṇy asur vyomavāyur agnir jalaṁ mahīsūryaś candras tapaś caivasthānāny agre kṛtāni te
verses.text: My dear boy, I have already selected the following places for your residence: the heart, the senses, the air of life, the sky, the air, the fire, the water, the earth, the sun, the moon and austerity.

verses.sanskrit: मन्युर्मनुर्महिनसो महाञ्छिव ऋतध्वज: ।उग्ररेता भव: कालो वामदेवो धृतव्रत: ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: manyur manur mahinasomahāñ chiva ṛtadhvajaḥugraretā bhavaḥ kālovāmadevo dhṛtavrataḥ
verses.text: Lord Brahmā said: My dear boy Rudra, you have eleven other names: Manyu, Manu, Mahinasa, Mahān, Śiva, Ṛtadhvaja, Ugraretā, Bhava, Kāla, Vāmadeva and Dhṛtavrata.

verses.sanskrit: धीर्धृतिरसलोमा च नियुत्सर्पिरिलाम्बिका ।इरावती स्वधा दीक्षा रुद्राण्यो रुद्र ते स्त्रिय: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhīr dhṛti-rasalomā caniyut sarpir ilāmbikāirāvatī svadhā dīkṣārudrāṇyo rudra te striyaḥ
verses.text: O Rudra, you also have eleven wives, called the Rudrāṇīs, and they are as follows: Dhī, Dhṛti, Rasalā, Umā, Niyut, Sarpi, Ilā, Ambikā, Irāvatī, Svadhā and Dīkṣā.

verses.sanskrit: गृहाणैतानि नामानि स्थानानि च सयोषण: ।एभि: सृज प्रजा बह्वी: प्रजानामसि यत्पति: ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: gṛhāṇaitāni nāmānisthānāni ca sa-yoṣaṇaḥebhiḥ sṛja prajā bahvīḥprajānām asi yat patiḥ
verses.text: My dear boy, you may now accept all the names and places designated for you and your different wives, and since you are now one of the masters of the living entities, you may increase the population on a large scale.

verses.sanskrit: इत्यादिष्ट: स्वगुरुणा भगवान्नीललोहित: ।सत्त्वाकृतिस्वभावेन ससर्जात्मसमा: प्रजा: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity ādiṣṭaḥ sva-guruṇābhagavān nīla-lohitaḥsattvākṛti-svabhāvenasasarjātma-samāḥ prajāḥ
verses.text: The most powerful Rudra, whose bodily color was blue mixed with red, created many offspring exactly resembling him in features, strength and furious nature.

verses.sanskrit: रुद्राणां रुद्रसृष्टानां समन्ताद् ग्रसतां जगत् ।निशाम्यासंख्यशो यूथान् प्रजापतिरशङ्कत ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: rudrāṇāṁ rudra-sṛṣṭānāṁsamantād grasatāṁ jagatniśāmyāsaṅkhyaśo yūthānprajāpatir aśaṅkata
verses.text: The sons and grandsons generated by Rudra were unlimited in number, and when they assembled together they attempted to devour the entire universe. When Brahmā, the father of the living entities, saw this, he became afraid of the situation.

verses.sanskrit: अलं प्रजाभि: सृष्टाभिरीद‍ृशीभि: सुरोत्तम ।मया सह दहन्तीभिर्दिशश्चक्षुर्भिरुल्बणै: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: alaṁ prajābhiḥ sṛṣṭābhirīdṛśībhiḥ surottamamayā saha dahantībhirdiśaś cakṣurbhir ulbaṇaiḥ
verses.text: Brahmā told Rudra: O best among the demigods, there is no need for you to generate living entities of this nature. They have begun to devastate everything on all sides with the fiery flames from their eyes, and they have even attacked me.

verses.sanskrit: तप आतिष्ठ भद्रं ते सर्वभूतसुखावहम् ।तपसैव यथापूर्व स्रष्टा विश्वमिदं भवान् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: tapa ātiṣṭha bhadraṁ tesarva-bhūta-sukhāvahamtapasaiva yathā pūrvaṁsraṣṭā viśvam idaṁ bhavān
verses.text: My dear son, you had better situate yourself in penance, which is auspicious for all living entities and which will bring all benediction upon you. By penance only shall you be able to create the universe as it was before.

verses.sanskrit: तपसैव परं ज्योतिर्भगवन्तमधोक्षजम् ।सर्वभूतगुहावासमञ्जसा विन्दते पुमान् ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tapasaiva paraṁ jyotirbhagavantam adhokṣajamsarva-bhūta-guhāvāsamañjasā vindate pumān
verses.text: By penance only can one even approach the Personality of Godhead, who is within the heart of every living entity and at the same time beyond the reach of all senses.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचएवमात्मभुवादिष्ट: परिक्रम्य गिरां पतिम् ।बाढमित्यमुमामन्‍त्र्य विवेश तपसे वनम् ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaevam ātmabhuvādiṣṭaḥparikramya girāṁ patimbāḍham ity amum āmantryaviveśa tapase vanam
verses.text: Śrī Maitreya said: Thus Rudra, having been ordered by Brahmā, circumambulated his father, the master of the Vedas. Addressing him with words of assent, he entered the forest to perform austere penances.

verses.sanskrit: अथाभिध्यायत: सर्गं दश पुत्रा: प्रजज्ञिरे ।भगवच्छक्तियुक्तस्य लोकसन्तानहेतव: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: athābhidhyāyataḥ sargaṁdaśa putrāḥ prajajñirebhagavac-chakti-yuktasyaloka-santāna-hetavaḥ
verses.text: Brahmā, who was empowered by the Supreme Personality of Godhead, thought of generating living entities and begot ten sons for the extension of the generations.

verses.sanskrit: मरीचिरत्र्याङ्गिरसौ पुलस्त्य: पुलह: क्रतु: ।भृगुर्वसिष्ठो दक्षश्च दशमस्तत्र नारद: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: marīcir atry-aṅgirasaupulastyaḥ pulahaḥ kratuḥbhṛgur vasiṣṭho dakṣaś cadaśamas tatra nāradaḥ
verses.text: Marīci, Atri, Aṅgirā, Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu, Bhṛgu, Vasiṣṭha, Dakṣa, and the tenth son, Nārada, were thus born.

verses.sanskrit: उत्सङ्गान्नारदो जज्ञे दक्षोऽङ्गुष्ठात्स्वयम्भुव: ।प्राणाद्वसिष्ठ: सञ्जातो भृगुस्त्वचि करात्क्रतु: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: utsaṅgān nārado jajñedakṣo ’ṅguṣṭhāt svayambhuvaḥprāṇād vasiṣṭhaḥ sañjātobhṛgus tvaci karāt kratuḥ
verses.text: Nārada was born from the deliberation of Brahmā, which is the best part of the body. Vasiṣṭha was born from his breathing, Dakṣa from a thumb, Bhṛgu from his touch, and Kratu from his hand.

verses.sanskrit: पुलहो नाभितो जज्ञे पुलस्त्य: कर्णयोऋर्षि: ।अङ्गिरा मुखतोऽक्ष्णोऽत्रिर्मरीचिर्मनसोऽभवत् ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: pulaho nābhito jajñepulastyaḥ karṇayor ṛṣiḥaṅgirā mukhato ’kṣṇo ’trirmarīcir manaso ’bhavat
verses.text: Pulastya was generated from the ears, Aṅgirā from the mouth, Atri from the eyes, Marīci from the mind and Pulaha from the navel of Brahmā.

verses.sanskrit: धर्म: स्तनाद्दक्षिणतो यत्र नारायण: स्वयम् ।अधर्म पृष्ठतो यस्मान्मृत्युर्लोकभयङ्कर: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: dharmaḥ stanād dakṣiṇatoyatra nārāyaṇaḥ svayamadharmaḥ pṛṣṭhato yasmānmṛtyur loka-bhayaṅkaraḥ
verses.text: Religion was manifested from the breast of Brahmā, wherein is seated the Supreme Personality of Godhead Nārāyaṇa, and irreligion appeared from his back, where horrible death takes place for the living entity.

verses.sanskrit: हृदि कामो भ्रुव: क्रोधो लोभश्चाधरदच्छदात् ।आस्याद्वाक्सिन्धवो मेढ्रान्निऋर्ति: पायोरघाश्रय: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: hṛdi kāmo bhruvaḥ krodholobhaś cādhara-dacchadātāsyād vāk sindhavo meḍhrānnirṛtiḥ pāyor aghāśrayaḥ
verses.text: Lust and desire became manifested from the heart of Brahmā, anger from between his eyebrows, greed from between his lips, the power of speaking from his mouth, the ocean from his penis, and low and abominable activities from his anus, the source of all sins.

verses.sanskrit: छायाया: कर्दमो जज्ञे देवहूत्या: पति: प्रभु: ।मनसो देहतश्चेदं जज्ञे विश्वकृतो जगत् ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: chāyāyāḥ kardamo jajñedevahūtyāḥ patiḥ prabhuḥmanaso dehataś cedaṁjajñe viśva-kṛto jagat
verses.text: Sage Kardama, husband of the great Devahūti, was manifested from the shadow of Brahmā. Thus all became manifested from either the body or the mind of Brahmā.

verses.sanskrit: वाचं दुहितरं तन्वीं स्वयम्भूर्हरतीं मन: ।अकामां चकमे क्षत्त: सकाम इति न: श्रुतम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: vācaṁ duhitaraṁ tanvīṁsvayambhūr haratīṁ manaḥakāmāṁ cakame kṣattaḥsa-kāma iti naḥ śrutam
verses.text: O Vidura, we have heard that Brahmā had a daughter named Vāk who was born from his body and who attracted his mind toward sex, although she was not sexually inclined towards him.

verses.sanskrit: तमधर्मे कृतमतिं विलोक्य पितरं सुता: ।मरीचिमुख्या मुनयो विश्रम्भात्प्रत्यबोधयन् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam adharme kṛta-matiṁvilokya pitaraṁ sutāḥmarīci-mukhyā munayoviśrambhāt pratyabodhayan
verses.text: Thus, finding their father so deluded in an act of immorality, the sages headed by Marīci, all sons of Brahmā, spoke as follows with great respect.

verses.sanskrit: नैतत्पूर्वै: कृतं त्वद्ये न करिष्यन्ति चापरे ।यस्त्वं दुहितरं गच्छेरनिगृह्याङ्गजं प्रभु: ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: naitat pūrvaiḥ kṛtaṁ tvad yena kariṣyanti cāpareyas tvaṁ duhitaraṁ gaccheranigṛhyāṅgajaṁ prabhuḥ
verses.text: O father, this performance in which you are endeavoring to complicate yourself was never attempted by any other Brahmā, nor by anyone else, nor by you in previous kalpas, nor will anyone dare to attempt it in the future. You are the supreme being in the universe, so how is it that you want to have sex with your daughter and cannot control your desire?

verses.sanskrit: तेजीयसामपि ह्येतन्न सुश्लोक्यं जगद्गुरो ।यद्‌वृत्तमनुतिष्ठन् वै लोक: क्षेमाय कल्पते ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: tejīyasām api hy etanna suślokyaṁ jagad-guroyad-vṛttam anutiṣṭhan vailokaḥ kṣemāya kalpate
verses.text: Even though you are the most powerful being, this act does not suit you because your character is followed for spiritual improvement by people in general.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मै नमो भगवते य इदं स्वेन रोचिषा ।आत्मस्थं व्यञ्जयामास स धर्मं पातुमर्हति ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmai namo bhagavateya idaṁ svena rociṣāātma-sthaṁ vyañjayām āsasa dharmaṁ pātum arhati
verses.text: Let us offer our respectful obeisances unto the Personality of Godhead, who, by His own effulgence, while situated in Himself, has manifested this cosmos. May He also protect religion for all goodness.

verses.sanskrit: स इत्थं गृणत: पुत्रान् पुरो दृष्ट्वा प्रजापतीन् ।प्रजापतिपतिस्तन्वं तत्याज व्रीडितस्तदा ।तां दिशो जगृहुर्घोरां नीहारं यद्विदुस्तम: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa itthaṁ gṛṇataḥ putrānpuro dṛṣṭvā prajāpatīnprajāpati-patis tanvaṁtatyāja vrīḍitas tadātāṁ diśo jagṛhur ghorāṁnīhāraṁ yad vidus tamaḥ
verses.text: The father of all Prajāpatis, Brahmā, thus seeing all his Prajāpati sons speaking in that way, became very much ashamed and at once gave up the body he had accepted. Later that body appeared in all directions as the dangerous fog in darkness.

verses.sanskrit: कदाचिद् ध्यायत: स्रष्टुर्वेदा आसंश्चतुर्मुखात् ।कथं स्रक्ष्याम्यहं लोकान् समवेतान् यथा पुरा ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: kadācid dhyāyataḥ sraṣṭurvedā āsaṁś catur-mukhātkathaṁ srakṣyāmy ahaṁ lokānsamavetān yathā purā
verses.text: Once upon a time, when Brahmā was thinking of how to create the worlds as in the past millennium, the four Vedas, which contain all varieties of knowledge, became manifested from his four mouths.

verses.sanskrit: चातुर्होत्रं कर्मतन्त्रमुपवेदनयै: सह ।धर्मस्य पादाश्चत्वारस्तथैवाश्रमवृत्तय: ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: cātur-hotraṁ karma-tantramupaveda-nayaiḥ sahadharmasya pādāś catvārastathaivāśrama-vṛttayaḥ
verses.text: The four kinds of paraphernalia for conducting the fire sacrifice became manifest: the performer [the chanter], the offerer, the fire, and the action performed in terms of the supplementary Vedas. Also the four principles of religiosity [truth, austerity, mercy and cleanliness] and the duties in the four social orders all became manifest.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाचस वै विश्वसृजामीशो वेदादीन् मुखतोऽसृजत् ।यद् यद् येनासृजद् देवस्तन्मे ब्रूहि तपोधन ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidura uvācasa vai viśva-sṛjām īśovedādīn mukhato ’sṛjatyad yad yenāsṛjad devastan me brūhi tapo-dhana
verses.text: Vidura said: O great sage whose only wealth is penance, kindly explain to me how and with whose help Brahmā established the Vedic knowledge which emanated from his mouth.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचऋग्यजु:सामाथर्वाख्यान् वेदान् पूर्वादिभिर्मुखै: ।शास्त्रमिज्यां स्तुतिस्तोमं प्रायश्चित्तं व्यधात्क्रमात् ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaṛg-yajuḥ-sāmātharvākhyānvedān pūrvādibhir mukhaiḥśāstram ijyāṁ stuti-stomaṁprāyaścittaṁ vyadhāt kramāt
verses.text: Maitreya said: Beginning from the front face of Brahmā, gradually the four Vedas — Ṛk, Yajur, Sāma and Atharva — became manifest. Thereafter, Vedic hymns which had not been pronounced before, priestly rituals, the subject matters of the recitation, and transcendental activities were all established, one after another.

verses.sanskrit: आयुर्वेदं धनुर्वेदं गान्धर्वं वेदमात्मन: ।स्थापत्यं चासृजद् वेदं क्रमात्पूर्वादिभिर्मुखै: ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: āyur-vedaṁ dhanur-vedaṁgāndharvaṁ vedam ātmanaḥsthāpatyaṁ cāsṛjad vedaṁkramāt pūrvādibhir mukhaiḥ
verses.text: He also created the medical science, military art, musical art and architectural science, all from the Vedas. They all emanated one after another, beginning from the front face.

verses.sanskrit: इतिहासपुराणानि पञ्चमं वेदमीश्वर: ।सर्वेभ्य एव वक्त्रेभ्य: ससृजे सर्वदर्शन: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: itihāsa-purāṇānipañcamaṁ vedam īśvaraḥsarvebhya eva vaktrebhyaḥsasṛje sarva-darśanaḥ
verses.text: Then he created the fifth Veda — the Purāṇas and the histories — from all his mouths, since he could see all the past, present and future.

verses.sanskrit: षोडश्युक्थौ पूर्ववक्त्रात्पुरीष्यग्निष्टुतावथ ।आप्तोर्यामातिरात्रौ च वाजपेयं सगोसवम् ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: ṣoḍaśy-ukthau pūrva-vaktrātpurīṣy-agniṣṭutāv athaāptoryāmātirātrau cavājapeyaṁ sagosavam
verses.text: All the different varieties of fire sacrifices [ṣoḍaśī, uktha, purīṣi, agniṣṭoma, āptoryāma, atirātra, vājapeya and gosava] became manifested from the eastern mouth of Brahmā.

verses.sanskrit: विद्या दानं तप: सत्यं धर्मस्येति पदानि च ।आश्रमांश्च यथासंख्यमसृजत्सह वृत्तिभि: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidyā dānaṁ tapaḥ satyaṁdharmasyeti padāni caāśramāṁś ca yathā-saṅkhyamasṛjat saha vṛttibhiḥ
verses.text: Education, charity, penance and truth are said to be the four legs of religion, and to learn this there are four orders of life with different classifications of castes according to vocation. Brahmā created all these in systematic order.

verses.sanskrit: सावित्रं प्राजापत्यं च ब्राह्मं चाथ बृहत्तथा ।वार्तासञ्चयशालीनशिलोञ्छ इति वै गृहे ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sāvitraṁ prājāpatyaṁ cabrāhmaṁ cātha bṛhat tathāvārtā sañcaya-śālīna-śiloñcha iti vai gṛhe
verses.text: Then the thread ceremony for the twice-born was inaugurated, as were the rules to be followed for at least one year after acceptance of the Vedas, rules for observing complete abstinence from sex life, vocations in terms of Vedic injunctions, various professional duties in household life, and the method of maintaining a livelihood without anyone’s cooperation by picking up rejected grains.

verses.sanskrit: वैखानसा वालखिल्यौदुम्बरा: फेनपा वने ।न्यासे कुटीचक: पूर्वं बह्वोदो हंसनिष्क्रियौ ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaikhānasā vālakhilyau-dumbarāḥ phenapā vanenyāse kuṭīcakaḥ pūrvaṁbahvodo haṁsa-niṣkriyau
verses.text: The four divisions of retired life are the vaikhānasas, vālakhilyas, audumbaras and phenapas. The four divisions of the renounced order of life are the kuṭīcakas, bahvodas, haṁsas and niṣkriyas. All these were manifested from Brahmā.

verses.sanskrit: आन्वीक्षिकी त्रयी वार्ता दण्डनीतिस्तथैव च ।एवं व्याहृतयश्वासन् प्रणवो ह्यस्य दहृत: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: ānvīkṣikī trayī vārtādaṇḍa-nītis tathaiva caevaṁ vyāhṛtayaś cāsanpraṇavo hy asya dahrataḥ
verses.text: The science of logical argument, the Vedic goals of life, and also law and order, moral codes and the celebrated hymns bhūḥ, bhuvaḥ and svaḥ all became manifested from the mouths of Brahmā, and the praṇava oṁkāra was manifested from his heart.

verses.sanskrit: तस्योष्णिगासील्लोमभ्यो गायत्री च त्वचो विभो: ।त्रिष्टुम्मांसात्स्‍नुतोऽनुष्टुब्जगत्यस्थ्न: प्रजापते: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasyoṣṇig āsīl lomabhyogāyatrī ca tvaco vibhoḥtriṣṭum māṁsāt snuto ’nuṣṭubjagaty asthnaḥ prajāpateḥ
verses.text: Thereafter the art of literary expression, uṣṇik, was generated from the hairs on the body of the almighty Prajāpati. The principal Vedic hymn, gāyatrī, was generated from the skin, triṣṭup from the flesh, anuṣṭup from the veins, and jagatī from the bones of the lord of the living entities.

verses.sanskrit: मज्जाया: पङ्‌क्तिरुत्पन्ना बृहती प्राणतोऽभवत् ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: majjāyāḥ paṅktir utpannābṛhatī prāṇato ’bhavat
verses.text: The art of writing verse, paṅkti, became manifested from the bone marrow, and that of bṛhatī, another type of verse, was generated from the life-breath of the lord of the living entities.

verses.sanskrit: स्पर्शस्तस्याभवज्जीव: स्वरो देह उदाहृत ।ऊष्माणमिन्द्रियाण्याहुरन्त:स्था बलमात्मन: ।स्वरा: सप्त विहारेण भवन्ति स्म प्रजापते: ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sparśas tasyābhavaj jīvaḥsvaro deha udāhṛtaūṣmāṇam indriyāṇy āhurantaḥ-sthā balam ātmanaḥsvarāḥ sapta vihāreṇabhavanti sma prajāpateḥ
verses.text: Brahmā’s soul was manifested as the touch alphabets, his body as the vowels, his senses as the sibilant alphabets, his strength as the intermediate alphabets and his sensual activities as the seven notes of music.

verses.sanskrit: शब्दब्रह्मात्मनस्तस्य व्यक्ताव्यक्तात्मन: पर: ।ब्रह्मावभाति विततो नानाशक्त्युपबृंहित: ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: śabda-brahmātmanas tasyavyaktāvyaktātmanaḥ paraḥbrahmāvabhāti vitatonānā-śakty-upabṛṁhitaḥ
verses.text: Brahmā is the personal representation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead as the source of transcendental sound and is therefore above the conception of manifested and unmanifested. Brahmā is the complete form of the Absolute Truth and is invested with multifarious energies.

verses.sanskrit: ततोऽपरामुपादाय स सर्गाय मनो दधे ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tato ’parām upādāyasa sargāya mano dadhe
verses.text: Thereafter Brahmā accepted another body, in which sex life was not forbidden, and thus he engaged himself in the matter of further creation.

verses.sanskrit: ऋषीणां भूरिवीर्याणामपि सर्गमविस्तृतम् ।ज्ञात्वा तद्‍धृदये भूयश्चिन्तयामास कौरव ॥ ५० ॥
verses.transliteration: ṛṣīṇāṁ bhūri-vīryāṇāmapi sargam avistṛtamjñātvā tad dhṛdaye bhūyaścintayām āsa kaurava
verses.text: O son of the Kurus, when Brahmā saw that in spite of the presence of sages of great potency there was no sufficient increase in population, he seriously began to consider how the population could be increased.

verses.sanskrit: अहो अद्भुतमेतन्मे व्यापृतस्यापि नित्यदा ।न ह्येधन्ते प्रजा नूनं दैवमत्र विघातकम् ॥ ५१ ॥
verses.transliteration: aho adbhutam etan mevyāpṛtasyāpi nityadāna hy edhante prajā nūnaṁdaivam atra vighātakam
verses.text: Brahmā thought to himself: Alas, it is wonderful that in spite of my being scattered all over, there is still insufficient population throughout the universe. There is no other cause for this misfortune but destiny.

verses.sanskrit: एवं युक्तकृतस्तस्य दैवञ्चावेक्षतस्तदा ।कस्य रूपमभूद् द्वेधा यत्कायमभिचक्षते ॥ ५२ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ yukta-kṛtas tasyadaivaṁ cāvekṣatas tadākasya rūpam abhūd dvedhāyat kāyam abhicakṣate
verses.text: While he was thus absorbed in contemplation and was observing the supernatural power, two other forms were generated from his body. They are still celebrated as the body of Brahmā.

verses.sanskrit: ताभ्यां रूपविभागाभ्यां मिथुनं समपद्यत ॥ ५३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tābhyāṁ rūpa-vibhāgābhyāṁmithunaṁ samapadyata
verses.text: The two newly separated bodies united together in a sexual relationship.

verses.sanskrit: यस्तु तत्र पुमान् सोऽभून्मनु: स्वायम्भुव: स्वराट् ।स्त्री याऽसीच्छतरूपाख्या महिष्यस्य महात्मन: ॥ ५४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yas tu tatra pumān so ’bhūnmanuḥ svāyambhuvaḥ svarāṭstrī yāsīc chatarūpākhyāmahiṣy asya mahātmanaḥ
verses.text: Out of them, the one who had the male form became known as the Manu named Svāyambhuva, and the woman became known as Śatarūpā, the queen of the great soul Manu.

verses.sanskrit: तदा मिथुनधर्मेण प्रजा ह्येधाम्बभूविरे ॥ ५५ ॥
verses.transliteration: tadā mithuna-dharmeṇaprajā hy edhām babhūvire
verses.text: Thereafter, by sex indulgence, they gradually increased generations of population one after another.

verses.sanskrit: स चापि शतरूपायां पञ्चापत्यान्यजीजनत् ।प्रियव्रतोत्तानपादौ तिस्र: कन्याश्च भारत ।आकूतिर्देवहूतिश्च प्रसूतिरिति सत्तम ॥ ५६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa cāpi śatarūpāyāṁpañcāpatyāny ajījanatpriyavratottānapādautisraḥ kanyāś ca bhārataākūtir devahūtiś caprasūtir iti sattama
verses.text: O son of Bharata, in due course of time he [Manu] begot in Śatarūpā five children — two sons, Priyavrata and Uttānapāda, and three daughters, Ākūti, Devahūti and Prasūti.

verses.sanskrit: आकूतिं रुचये प्रादात्कर्दमाय तु मध्यमाम् ।दक्षायादात्प्रसूतिं च यत आपूरितं जगत् ॥ ५७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ākūtiṁ rucaye prādātkardamāya tu madhyamāmdakṣāyādāt prasūtiṁ cayata āpūritaṁ jagat
verses.text: The father, Manu, handed over his first daughter, Ākūti, to the sage Ruci, the middle daughter, Devahūti, to the sage Kardama, and the youngest, Prasūti, to Dakṣa. From them, all the world filled with population.
